sales.description:
CLAUDIUS, A.D. 41-54. AR Denarius (3.81 gms), Rome Mint, A.D. 51-52. NGC VF, Strike: 4/5 Surface: 4/5.
To view all items from the John P. Burnham Collection, click  here.
RIC-62; RSC-68. Obverse: Laureate bust right; Reverse: Pax-Nemesis advancing right; holding drapery below chin and wing caduceus, point down at snake gliding rightwards. An impressive example of a SCARCE type, yielding deeply gunmetal surfaces, and impressive strike, with moderate circulation giving this Denarius a charming h...

sales.title:
Heritage World Coin Auctions - NYINC Signature Sale 3129, Lot 34046 — Ancients
Nero Claudius Drusus (38-9 BC). AR denarius (20mm, 3.82 gm, 1h). NGC AU★ 5/5 - 4/5. Posthumous issue of Rome, AD 41-45. NERO CLAVDIVS DRVSVS GERMANICVS IMP, laureate head of Drusus left / DE
sales.description:
Ancients
Nero Claudius Drusus (38-9 BC). AR denarius (20mm, 3.82 gm, 1h). NGC AU★ 5/5 - 4/5. Posthumous issue of Rome, AD 41-45. NERO CLAVDIVS DRVSVS GERMANICVS IMP, laureate head of Drusus left / DE GERMANIS on architrave of triumphal arch surmounted by equestrian statue to left between two trophies. RIC I (Claudius I) 72. Breathtakingly handsome portrait, complemented by fiery peripheral toning on a large flan. Very rare and among the finest specimens known.

From the Mirabilis Collection. E...
